Scenario.overview: Communications Complex A sophisticated multi-stage attack targeting a communications provider, leveraging phishing, lateral movement, and exfiltration to disrupt operations and steal sensitive data.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Financial gain through data extortion and operational disruption

Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: ["Internet-facing assets", "Weak passwords", "Improper configuration", "Vulnerability", "Shared DB or directory", "Public and private cloud", "Web server services such as emails and databases"]
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: ["Phishing", "Vulnerability exploit", "Ransomware", "Malware", "Brute-force attack", "Credential compromise", "Web", "Security misconfigurations and vulnerabilities"]

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: Adversaries gathered public domain registration details, employee information, and infrastructure configurations from WHOIS records and social media to identify potential vulnerabilities in the target's communication systems.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The attackers compromised a third-party cloud hosting service to deploy phishing landing pages and malicious payloads, masking their origin to avoid direct attribution.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2019-2215 [CWE-502]: Apache Log4j2 JNDI features used in configuration...
stage.description: A spear-phishing campaign delivered macro-enabled documents via email to employees. When opened, the malicious attachment exploited a vulnerability in Apache Log4j2 (CVE-2019-2215) to establish initial access to the victim's network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attackers executed arbitrary code by exploiting an unpatched Microsoft Office vulnerability (CVE-2017-0263) on compromised endpoints to deploy a backdoor.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: A scheduled task was created in the Windows Task Scheduler to ensure periodic execution of the backdoor, maintaining persistent access to the network.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: Weak permissions on SIP/VoIP services were exploited to escalate privileges, granting the attackers administrative access to critical communication infrastructure.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.surface: Web server services such as emails and databases
stage.description: Malicious code was injected into legitimate communication processes (e.g., Outlook, Teams) to evade detection by security tools.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: Credentials were extracted from memory using Mimikatz, enabling access to additional accounts across the network.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: Internal network scans identified critical communication nodes, including PBX systems and email servers, for further exploitation.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 555: Remote Services with Stolen Credentials
stage.description: Stolen credentials were used to brute-force RDP access, allowing lateral movement across the network to reach high-value targets.

stage.name: Collection
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 168: Windows ::DATA Alternate Data Stream
stage.description: Sensitive communication data, including voicemail backups and email archives, were exfiltrated using alternate data streams to avoid detection.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 608: Cryptanalysis of Cellular Encryption
stage.description: Stolen data was transferred via HTTPS and DNS tunneling to evade network monitoring and ensure secure exfiltration.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 325: TCP Congestion Control Flag (ECN) Probe
stage.description: Ransomware was deployed to encrypt critical communication systems, followed by DDoS attacks to disrupt operations and demand payment.

Scenario.summary: The C0017 campaign executed a complex attack chain starting with spear-phishing to exploit Apache Log4j2 and Microsoft Office vulnerabilities, escalating privileges through misconfigured services, and disrupting communications via ransomware and DDoS. The attack leveraged both technical exploits and social engineering to achieve persistence and data exfiltration.
